key_insight: The paper surveys finite-strain constitutive models for magneto-active polymers, showing how modelling has evolved from semi-empirical laws to thermodynamically consistent nonlinear frameworks that capture anisotropy, viscoelasticity, and field-induced stiffening.
engineering_relevance: These models support the design of adaptive soft devices such as sensors, actuators, vibration-control components, and soft robotic systems that can be tuned by magnetic fields.
method: Reviews isotropic and anisotropic magnetoelastic constitutive models.
Compares invariant-based, spectral, variational, polyconvex, microstructural, and magneto-viscoelastic formulations.
Summarizes coupling mechanisms, state variables, and physical assumptions used across model families.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a structured survey of existing theory and experimental behaviors rather than a new dataset or benchmark evaluation.
watch_item: A key implementation concern is reliable parameter identification and validation, especially when thermo-magneto-mechanical coupling and microstructure-sensitive effects are important.
paper_title: Constitutive modelling of magneto-active polymers at finite strains: A survey
